title: Firmware 4.2: Sleep Staging, Readiness 2.0, and Three Small Fixes We're Proud Of
excerpt: The biggest Lumivex update this year: REM detection, deep sleep tracking, and a readiness score that actually makes sense.
author: Sofia Reyes
content:
Firmware 4.2 started shipping December 15th. If you haven't updated yet, open the Lumivex app and tap the notification — it takes about 8 minutes. Here's what's new and why we're excited about it.

Sleep Staging: REM and Deep Sleep, Finally

Until now, Lumivex tracked total sleep duration, awake time, and light sleep. We knew REM and deep sleep staging was the #1 feature request — it's been on our roadmap since day one. The delay wasn't hardware (our sensors were capable). It was validation.

We refused to ship sleep staging until we could prove it was accurate. Our benchmark was polysomnography (PSG) — the gold-standard sleep lab test with EEG, EOG, and EMG sensors. Against PSG in a 200-person study, Firmware 4.2 achieves:

  • Wake detection: 94% agreement with PSG
  • REM detection: 87% agreement
  • Deep sleep: 85% agreement
  • Light sleep: 82% agreement

Those numbers aren't perfect — no wrist-worn device matches a sleep lab. But they're good enough to give you a meaningful picture of your sleep architecture. You'll see your nightly breakdown in the app starting today.

Readiness Score 2.0

Honest admission: Readiness 1.0 was too sensitive. A single bad night of sleep could drop your score 30 points, which made the metric feel random. Version 2.0 takes a rolling 7-day weighted average, with more weight on recent nights but enough smoothing that one bad night doesn't tank your score.

The new algorithm also incorporates HRV trend direction — not just the absolute value. A rising HRV over 3 days is a positive recovery signal even if the absolute number is below your average. That nuance makes the score much more actionable.

Three Small Fixes

  1. SpO2 sampling rate: Nighttime SpO2 now samples every 5 minutes instead of 15. Battery impact is negligible (0.3% per night).
  2. GPS lock time: Cold start GPS acquisition is ~40% faster thanks to better satellite ephemeris caching.
  3. Haptic strength: Added a "Strong" haptic option in Settings for people who found the default too subtle.

Update your watch tonight. Tomorrow morning, check your first sleep stage breakdown. It's wild to actually see your REM cycles.
metaTitle: Firmware 4.2: Sleep Staging & Readiness 2.0 | Lumivex Blog
metaDescription: Lumivex Firmware 4.2 adds REM/deep sleep staging, a smarter readiness score, faster GPS lock, and stronger haptics. Shipping now.

faq.question: How do I update my firmware?
faq.answer: Open the Lumivex app, go to Settings → Device → Firmware Update. Ensure your watch has at least 30% battery. The update takes about 8 minutes.

faq.question: Will this update reset my health data?
faq.answer: No. All health data, settings, and preferences are preserved through firmware updates.
